content:
🚀 Tamari | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: A personal web application for users to manage, organize, and discover recipes, plan meals, and create shopping lists.
✨ Key Features: • User account creation and management • Recipe storage, viewing, searching, and sharing • Recipe organization (categories, favorites) • Recipe import from external websites • Shopping list creation and management
📖 Summary: Tamari is a comprehensive web application for managing recipes, built with Python and Flask. It allows users to store, search, organize, and share recipes, create shopping lists, plan meals, and import recipes from other websites.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 tookie-osint | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Discovering social media usernames across various websites based on a given input.
✨ Key Features: • Advanced OSINT information gathering • Finds social media accounts based on inputs • Simple-to-use UI • High performance (rewritten from scratch) • Optimized for Python 3.12
📖 Summary: Tookie-OSINT is an advanced Python-based tool designed for efficient information gathering, specifically focused on discovering social media accounts based on provided inputs. It aims to be user-friendly and highly performant, assisting new programmers and pentesters in the field of OSINT.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 bore | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Exposing local services to the internet through a secure tunnel, enabling remote access or public testing of local applications.
✨ Key Features: • Simple CLI tool • TCP tunneling • Exposes local ports to remote servers • Bypasses NAT and firewalls • Efficient and unopinionated
📖 Summary: bore is a Rust-based CLI tool for creating simple TCP tunnels. It allows users to expose local ports to a remote server, effectively bypassing NAT and firewalls, and is designed for efficiency and ease of self-hosting.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 badapple_in_tg | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable users to play video animations within Telegram chats by converting videos into custom animated emoji sticker packs.
✨ Key Features: • Video to animated emoji sticker pack conversion • Playing animations (e.g., Bad Apple) in Telegram chats using custom emoji • Multi-bot architecture to bypass Telegram API limits • Customizable grid size for emoji animation
📖 Summary: This repository provides two Python bots for Telegram: a "cutter bot" that converts videos into animated emoji sticker packs, and a "player bot" that plays animations like Bad Apple using these custom emoji in Telegram chats. It leverages multiple bots to overcome Telegram API rate limits.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ codevault | PHP
🎯 Primary Use Case: A personal code library for developers to store, organize, search, and share code snippets.
✨ Key Features: • Snippet management with syntax highlighting • Full-text search across title, tags, and code • Public and private snippet options • Explore page for public snippets • User profiles
📖 Summary: CodeVault is a PHP-based web application designed to help developers manage their code snippets. It allows users to save, organize, search, and share code snippets with features like syntax highlighting, full-text search, public/private options, and a REST API.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Detours | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: Monitoring and instrumenting API calls on Windows for debugging, analysis, or modifying application behavior.
✨ Key Features: • API call monitoring • API call instrumentation • Hooking Windows API calls • Source code availability • MIT License
📖 Summary: Detours is a C++ software package for monitoring and instrumenting API calls on Windows. It allows developers to intercept and modify the behavior of existing Windows API functions, enabling features like debugging, performance analysis, and application behavior modification. The project is now open-sourced under the MIT license.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
